channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump’s Approval Sinks Amid Unpopular War, Darkening G.O.P. Prospects
item.description: With the midterms nearing, President Trump’s approval rating has hit a second-term low as voters question his handling of the economy, according to the latest New York Times/Siena poll.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: A Crack in the Polling Floor Puts Trump in New Territory
item.description: A 37 percent approval rating in a new Times/Siena poll suggests the G.O.P. is facing a big midterm problem despite recent redistricting gains.
item.dc:creator: Nate Cohn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tom Brenner for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump’s approval rating is his lowest recorded in a Times/Siena survey.

item.title: The Supreme Court’s Voting Rights Decision Spurred Swift Action in Louisiana and Alabama
item.description: The timing brought the court into the middle of a fight to redraw voting maps across the South, even as some primaries were underway.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The timing of the Supreme Court’s decision has complicated voting maps in several states, even as some had begun their primary process.

item.title: Trump’s Approval Rating Hits New Low, and Ebola Outbreak Declared a Global Health Emergency
item.description: Plus, inside the closed-door talks over Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Will Jarvis, Margaret Kadifa, Jake Lucas, Ian Stewart, Tracy Mumford and Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: With the midterms nearing, President Trump’s approval rating has hit a second-term low as voters question his handling of the economy, according to the latest New York Times/Siena poll.

item.title: L.I.R.R. Riders Stranded by Strike Have Few Commute Alternatives. Here’s What To Know.
item.description: New York transit officials say the alternate travel options they’ve arranged for the Long Island Rail Road strike could not accommodate all commuters.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen
item.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
item.media:description: Long Island Rail Road service has been shut down since Saturday.

item.title: In Closed-Door Talks, U.S. Demands a Major Role in Greenland
item.description: Greenlandic officials worry about the direction of the negotiations aimed at defusing President Trump’s threats to seize their island. But they have little leverage.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman, Maya Tekeli, Anton Troianovski and Eric Schmitt
item.media:credit: Ivor Prickett for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ilulisaat, Greenland, a town where a Chinese state company nearly won a contract to build an airport in 2018. After U.S. officials pressured Denmark to step in, Greenland opted for a Danish company.

item.title: Brookings Institution Report: Over 100,000 Family Separations in Trump Crackdown
item.description: The Brookings Institution suggests that federal statistics are an undercount because immigrant parents are not being asked about or not disclosing their American children.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan and Jeff Adelson
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: Children have been separated from their parents, according to a new report, as the Trump administration continues its deportation push.

item.title: Catastrophe Is Emerging in the World’s Most Vulnerable Places
item.description: The humanitarian relief system, decimated by cuts, faces a grave challenge as the Middle East war causes soaring costs for food, fuel and fertilizer.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Goodman and Finbarr O’Reilly
item.category: United States Agency for International Development
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Muslima Ibrahim Mohamed, 38, holds her 2-day-old son, Noor Mohamed.

item.title: How the Music of ‘Schmigadoon!’ Echoes Broadway’s Past
item.description: The composer Cinco Paul discusses the clever references to classic musicals everywhere you look (and hear) in his new Broadway show.
item.dc:creator: Alexis Soloski
item.media:description: “Schmigadoon!,” a musical satire adapted from the 2021 AppleTV+ series, is now playing at the Nederlander Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: What to Know About the Latest Ebola Outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization declared a global health emergency after about 80 people died from the virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:description: A motorcycle taxi driver at a hospital in Goma, Democratic Republic of Congo, on Sunday.

item.title: Americans Are Among Those Affected by Ebola Outbreak, C.D.C. Says
item.description: A small number of U.S. citizens have been directly affected by the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, the C.D.C. said on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
United States Agency for International Development
item.media:description: People wait near an ambulance at a hospital in Bunia, Congo on Sunday.

item.title: Blocked Strait of Hormuz Leads to New Opportunities for Syria
item.description: The latest war in the Middle East has created new economic opportunities for Syria thanks to its geography.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iraqi trucks carrying oil along the highway near the Baniyas port and refinery in Syria in April.

item.title: G7 Finance Ministers Look to Contain Iran Economic Fallout
item.description: Top policymakers were expected to discuss rising energy prices and sanctions policy at a critical summit in Paris this week.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, greeting Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent last week. Mr. Bessent is leading the U.S. delegation at the G7 finance ministers’ meeting.

item.title: Oil Prices Climb and Bonds Falter as Iran War Raises Inflation Fears
item.description: Stocks and bonds wavered after President Trump issued a new warning to Iran.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Actually, Democracy Dies in H.R.
item.description: New research sheds light on how mediocre employees help would-be authoritarians maintain power.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Taub
item.media:description: Argentine security forces seizing a young man in 1982 in Buenos Aires during the Dirty War.

item.title: NextEra Energy to Acquire Dominion, Creating a Utility Giant
item.description: The deal combining the utility companies in Florida and Virginia comes as demand for power is soaring, largely because of the rapid growth of A.I. data centers.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch and Ivan Penn
item.media:description: A wind farm owned by NextEra Energy in Texas. Peak electricity demand in coming summers and winters is expected to rise more than 20 percent nationwide by 2035.

item.title: He Shut Liquor Stores and Banned Abortion, All for the Glory of Russia
item.description: A firebrand governor aims to transform his region into a laboratory for the Kremlin’s reactionary ideals.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko and Mary Gelman
item.media:description: A mural reading “the powerhouse of the Russian world” in the city of Vologda, Russia, in February.

item.title: Josh Tyrangiel book excerpt: How OpenAI and Khan Academy Made a Chatbot
item.description: Inside a collaboration to bring artificial intelligence into the classroom.
item.dc:creator: Josh Tyrangiel
item.media:credit: Photo illustration by Pablo Delcan and Danielle Del Plato

item.title: When Tornado Weather Hits, These Scientists Break Out the Colored Pencils
item.description: With a battery of modern technology at their fingertips, meteorologists often turn first to an old-fashioned tracking technique.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones and Desiree Rios

item.title: Severe Weather, Including Hail and Tornadoes, Could Rattle the U.S. Midwest
item.description: Some of the most severe weather in days could hit Monday afternoon, meteorologists said. Tornadoes and large hail are possible.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCann and Nazaneen Ghaffar

item.title: MAGA Women Are Leading a #Me2.0 in Washington
item.description: These G.O.P. crusaders might be the perfect people to fight sexual misconduct on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Cottle
item.media:credit: Emily Elconin for The New York Times

item.title: The Fighting Never Ends
item.description: We look at what happens when countries say they’re going to stop shooting at each other.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The site of an Israeli airstrike in Beirut’s southern suburbs this month.

item.title: The Quest for Clean Hydrogen Moves Underground
item.description: The dream of clean hydrogen has tantalized energy experts for years, but producing it has been tough. Many start-ups think the answer could lie beneath our feet.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer and Ian Willms

item.title: Mamdani’s First City-Owned Grocery Store Is Planned for the South Bronx
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani plans to announce that the store will open in the Hunts Point neighborhood next year. Some merchants are pushing back.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons
item.media:credit: David Dee Delgado for The New York Times
item.media:description: The proposed location of Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s first city-owned grocery store in the Hunts Point neighborhood of the Bronx.

item.title: Soccer Star Kylian Mbappé Faces Off Against France’s Far Right
item.description: Kylian Mbappé, the captain of France’s national team, set off a storm a month before the World Cup by suggesting that the National Rally party was a threat to France.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.media:description: “I know what it means, and what kind of consequences it can have for my country when those kinds of people take control,” Kylian Mbappé said of the French far-right in a magazine interview.

item.title: Insurers’ Delays in Approving Medical Care Persist, Despite Promises
item.description: Doctors and patients complain that the controversial practice of prior authorization for treatment and procedures is still widespread.
item.dc:creator: Reed Abelson
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield Assn
item.media:credit: Lindsay D'Addato for The New York Times
item.media:description: Candace Rond, center, with her daughters Gabby, left, and Sophia, who both have juvenile arthritis.
